page: Amazon Web Services x8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as Gzip and Bzip3, also perform in the top 25 percent. For machine learning workloads, the hardware delivers top-tier text generation speeds of 372.71 tokens per second when running small 135M parameter LLM models, although performance scales to average levels for larger 7B models.

The server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be handled via network-attached options. Network connectivity is rated at an 8 Gbps baseline. Given its massive memory capacity and dedicated CPU allocation, the instance offers high resource density for memory-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, text processing, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed, paired with 256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. While the instance lacks local storage and GPUs, it provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong single-threaded decompression speeds. This hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and complex text processing pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
256.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Designed for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing
